dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8880 : Small Business Cybersecurity Assistance Evaluation Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Received; read twice and referred to the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs

form.official-title: To require the Comptroller General to evaluate Federal cybersecurity assistance to small business concerns, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Small Business Cybersecurity Assistance Evaluation Act of 2026.

section.header: GAO study on small business cybersecurity assistance

subsection.text: The Comptroller General of the United States shall conduct a study of current Federal cybersecurity initiatives, programs, resources, tools, and services intended to assist owners of small business concerns (as defined under section 3 of the Small Business Act (15 U.S.C. 632)) with—

paragraph.text: identifying cyber risks, cyber threats, and cybersecurity vulnerabilities relating to such concerns;
assessing the preparedness of such concerns for such risks, threats, and vulnerabilities;

planning for, mitigating, and recovering from cyberattacks and incidents of social engineering, scams, and fraud (including developing, adopting, and implementing cybersecurity measures, training, protocols, tools, and infrastructure); and
identifying sources of capital, or obtaining capital, to carry out the activities specified in paragraphs (1), (2), and (3).

subsection.text: The study required by subsection (a) shall include—

paragraph.text: information on the most common cyberattacks affecting small business concerns;
an identification and description of the Federal cybersecurity initiatives, programs, resources, tools, and services included in the study described in subsection (a);

an assessment of the awareness and use of such Federal cybersecurity initiatives, programs, resources, tools, and services by small business concerns and reasons for differences in levels of such awareness and use;
an assessment of the coordination and integration among such Federal cybersecurity initiatives, programs, resources, tools, and services;

an assessment of the effectiveness of such Federal cybersecurity initiatives, programs, resources, tools, and services in assisting small business concerns with the activities listed in paragraphs (1) through (4) of subsection (a);
an identification of any foundational cybersecurity concepts absent from such Federal cybersecurity initiatives, programs, resources, tools, and services; and
recommendations on how to improve the effectiveness, awareness, and coordination of such Federal cybersecurity initiatives, programs, resources, tools, and services for small business concerns.

subsection.text: The Comptroller General shall submit to the Committee on Small Business of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Small Business and Entrepreneurship of the Senate a report containing all findings and determinations made in carrying out the study required under subsection (a).

section.text: No such funds are authorized to be appropriated to carry out this Act.

attestation-group.attestation-date: Passed the House of Representatives June 23, 2026.
